findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "find_markets" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Free-text market/event lookup by team, league, or market title. This is the AGENT FRONT DOOR: call find_markets FIRST to resolve a team or league name (e.g. 'Ya…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

name: get_spreads
description: Cross-venue price-gap view over matched pairs whose resolution rule marks them spread-only (never arb-fireable) but that carry a live, disclosed cross-venue price gap. Only verified-band (EXACT/HIGH) rows are returned — this tool inherits the /v2/spreads band allowlist verbatim and never re-filters

name: get_coverage
description: Internal coverage census for the matched feed. Most integrations want find_markets instead.

name: report_bad_arb
description: Flag a bad or incorrect arbitrage opportunity for operator review. Include at least one of: opp_id, reason, sport, market, or detail. Mirrors POST /v2/report-bad-arb. Free to any active key.

name: watch_markets
description: Mint a short-lived (~120s) connect ticket for the real-time WebSocket market stream and return the ws_url plus your tier's allowed channels. The agent (or a client SDK) opens the WebSocket; this tool does not hold the connection. Your tier's allowed channels come back in `allowed.channels` (the sour

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "list_markets" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: List the matched, unified cross-venue catalog as canonical market objects. Each object has id, slug, title, sport, league, status, signal (spread|arb), spread_p…

evidence.snippet: Free-text market/event lookup by team, league, or market title. This is the AGENT FRONT DOOR: call find_markets FIRST to resolve a team or league name (e.g. 'Ya…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_markets, find_arbitrage, list_leagues, get_coverage, watch_markets · Public sink: report_bad_arb.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_markets, find_arbitrage, list_leagues, get_coverage, watch_markets · Public sink: report_bad_arb

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
